name: Max Dreyfus
abstract: Max Dreyfus (April 1, 1874 – May 12, 1964) was a German-born American music publisher, arranger and songwriter. Between the 1910s and 1950s he encouraged and published the work of many of the writers of the so-called Great American Songbook, and was president of Chappell & Co., Inc.

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: And they had a guy called Max Dreyfus who was famous — I mean he was an accomplished pianist, brilliant musician.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
